steps.title: Use the introductory clause
steps.content: The sentence begins with a dependent clause: Although most meteorites are fragments of asteroids. The words after the blank form the independent clause that completes the thought.

steps.title: Reject stronger punctuation
steps.content: Choice A turns the although clause into a fragment. Choice C uses a semicolon, but the first part is not an independent clause. Choice D uses a colon where a dependent clause simply needs to lead into the main clause. The answer is B.
